rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM PEARSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Consolidate Actions For All Purposes Including Trial
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 31, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiff¿s motion to consolidate actions for all purposes including trial is granted. No opposition filed. Cases numbered 274590 and 275896 are consolidated. Henceforth all papers shall be filed under the lead case no. 274590. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE UDELHOVEN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 31, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Curtiss-Wright Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of production. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declarations of plaintiff and Mr. Cohen are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HARVEY NULL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 31, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Industrial Electric Service Co.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting his claims against defendant. By failing to demonstrate with its moving papers that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his claims against defendant, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s objections to defendant¿s General Order 129 interrogatory responses are sustained. Interrogatory responses may only be used against the responding party. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: HARVEY NULL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 31, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to the issue of whether plaintiff was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony and responses to defendant¿s contention discovery, which identify plaintiff working with Square D products that contained asbestos, preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to the issue of substantial factor causation, defendant sustained its initial burden. Dr. Schonfeld¿s declaration creates a triable issue whether plaintiff¿s exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff¿s asbestos-related illness. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Schonfeld¿s declaration are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: AUBREY MATTHEWS VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 31, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 2 (general negligence excluding negligent failure to warn, strict liability design and manufacturing defect), denied as to issue 4 (punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 3 (false representation). Defendant satisfied its burden of production of making a prima facie case that it is entitled to prevail on its government contractor defense as to non-duty to warn claims and plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue on this defense. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not now possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiff¿s responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff represents that he is dismissing his false representation cause of action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LAVON HOELMER et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 31, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 11. Defendant Groeniger & Company¿s demurrer: Defendant¿s demurrer to the survival claims and loss of consortium cause of action is sustained without leave to amend based on res judicata. Plaintiff LaVon Hoelmer¿s claims as successor-in-interest to Mr. Hoelmer and her loss of consortium claim are based on the same primary rights as in the prior action, and in that action Mr. and Mrs. Hoelmer failed to amend their complaint after defendant¿s demurrer was sustained with leave to amend. Defendant¿s general demurrer to the claims that remain ¿ the claims brought by all plaintiffs based on wrongful death ¿ is sustained with ten days leave to amend to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
